pending.body: In `json_fill_none` (fs.rs:2284-2300), the `entry().or_insert(value)` merge silently prefers base over overlay for ALL fields, including any future optional fields that may need conflict detection. Currently, conflict checks for run_id, plan_hash, and outcome_detail are performed explicitly before calling `json_fill_none`, but if a future optional field with conflict semantics is added to StartJournalDetails or CompletionJournalDetails, the merge will silently keep the stale base value without flagging the conflict. Add a code comment at the `json_fill_none` call sites (fs.rs:2326 and fs.rs:2367) documenting the invariant: any new optional field with conflict semantics MUST have an explicit conflict check added to `merge()` above the `json_fill_none` call, otherwise conflicting values will be silently preserved from the existing record rather than flagged as an error.

run_snapshot.status_summary: running: final review restart round 8 -> Plan and Implement
